task_completion.description: Calculate the base area for each material, standardizing units to square feet.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A column showing the calculated area in square feet for the materials. The waterproof membrane (160x142 cm) must be converted to approximately 24.46 to 24.53 sq ft.

task_completion.description: Incorporate waste factors into the material quantities.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A column showing adjusted areas/quantities that add a percentage for waste (e.g., 10-15% for tiles, 5-10% for other materials) to the base area.

task_completion.description: Calculate the required number of packages using round-up logic.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A column showing the number of packages as whole numbers, calculated by dividing the adjusted area by the coverage per package and rounding up (e.g., Floor Tile should be 3 packages).

task_completion.description: Calculate the total cost for each material based on the required packages.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A column showing the total cost, which should equal the number of rounded packages multiplied by the price per package (e.g., Floor Tile cost should be $128.97).

task_completion.description: Flag any budget overruns by comparing total costs to the allocated budgets.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A column, text summary, or conditional formatting that explicitly checks or indicates whether the total cost of any item exceeds its budget (e.g., a 'Budget Overrun?' column with Yes/No or True/False values).

integrity.description: The agent used a genuine spreadsheet application or wrote a script to perform the calculations.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a spreadsheet application interface (e.g., LibreOffice Calc) or a terminal running a data script, not just a plain text editor with the final typed answers.

integrity.description: The agent actually calculated the values rather than hardcoding the expected answers from the prompt.
integrity.visual_evidence: The formula bar in the spreadsheet (if visible) shows formulas (e.g., =ROUNDUP(...), =A2*B2) rather than hardcoded final numbers, or the script contains the calculation logic.

integrity.description: The agent processed all materials, not just the examples provided in the instructions.
integrity.visual_evidence: All 6 materials (floor tile, wall tile, waterproof membrane, paint, tile adhesive, grout) are present in the final results, not just the Floor Tile and Waterproof Membrane examples.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires calculating material quantities and costs for 6 items. Key verified calculations to look for: 1) Waterproof membrane area must be converted from cm to sq ft (160x142 cm = ~24.46 to 24.53 sq ft). 2) Floor tile adjusted area (with 15% waste) = ~28.20 sq ft. 3) Floor tile packages = ROUNDUP(28.20 / 12) = 3 packages. 4) Floor tile total cost = 3 * $42.99 = $128.97. 5) Waterproof membrane packages = ROUNDUP(~26.90 / 107.64) = 1 package. 6) Waterproof membrane total cost = 1 * $89.99 = $89.99. Waste factors should be applied (typically 10-15% for tile, 5-10% for others). The final results must include a check or flag for budget overruns comparing total cost to the budget for each item.
